prompt: Develop a LearnMart Offline Training Commerce & Classroom system for a US-based professional training provider that sells paid courses and optional printed materials from the same Android device with no internet dependency. Users include Administrator (device owner who sets policies and manages backups), Registrar (enrollment approvals and seat control), Instructor and TA (teaching operations and grading), Learner (shopping, enrollment, coursework), and Finance Clerk (payment recording, refunds, and reconciliation). The app provides a course catalog where staff can create/publish/unpublish courses, open/close classes, schedule sessions with time slots, ordering, and a hard capacity (e.g., 25 seats) and assign instructors/TAs; learners can request enrollment under configurable rules (eligibility flags, priority tiers, waitlist enabled) and a multi-level approval flow that supports serial or parallel approvals, exceptions for over-capacity, and auto-expiration of pending requests after 48 hours with an auditable replay of each decision. Learners can add course fees and physical materials to a cart and check out under configurable policies such as “same-class only” vs “cross-class allowed,” $25.00 minimum order, $1.50 packaging fee, and real-time totals including discounts, sales tax, and service fee; order submission must be idempotent (same client-generated id within 5 minutes returns the original order) with price validation and a pre-order inventory lock that expires after 10 minutes and rolls back on cancellation. Payments are strictly offline: the app records tender types (cash, check, “external card terminal reference”), supports importing a locally provided settlement file (CSV/JSON) for signature-verified reconciliation, handles idempotent “payment status updates” from file imports, and supports refunds to the original recorded method including partial refunds down to $0.01; discrepancies must be flagged with required notes and a resolution status. Order lifecycle covers placement, payment, fulfillment, delivery confirmation, and refund/return/exchange with an explicit state machine and timeout automation (auto-cancel unpaid orders after 30 minutes; auto-close “awaiting pickup” after 7 days). Assignments and quizzes support question banks with difficulty and knowledge tags, timed release windows, on-device submission, basic plagiarism detection using local similarity/fingerprint matching across stored submissions, auto-grading for objective questions, and routed grading queues for subjective responses with wrong-answer tracking linked to explanations. All data is stored on-device in encrypted SQLite, using SQLDelight for type-safe queries and Room for transactional persistence where entity relations are heavy; no server-side logic, remote database, or third-party services are permitted. The UI is built with Jetpack Compose, with Hilt for dependency injection and ViewModel/UseCase boundaries to keep business rules testable offline; large catalogs and order histories must scroll at 60fps, using RecyclerView with DiffUtil (embedded where needed) to avoid full refresh on high-churn lists. Database reliability requires composite indexes (e.g., userId+createdAt for orders, courseId+sessionTime for schedules), consistent transactions for inventory locks and ledger writes, and reconciliation runs that are atomic with rollback on parse errors. File import/export, bulk export, and backup/restore operate via scoped storage on Android 10+ with format validation, field mapping, and data cleansing rules (trim, de-dup by primary keys, reject invalid dates); backups must be AES-256 encrypted and restorable without network access, with local access control, call logs for integrations (e.g., ContentProvider/Intent-based exchange), and rate/risk controls such as refund limits (max 3 refunds per learner per day), blacklist flags, and optional device fingerprinting stored locally. Performance and stability require coroutines to keep IO and computation off the main thread, WorkManager jobs for reconciliation/backup only when idle or charging, and image downsampling plus an LRU cache to keep peak image memory under 20MB to prevent OOM.
